description:
You are working as a FinOps analyst who has just received July-2023 multi-cloud cost exports.  
 The files are already present on the system in  

   /home/user/raw_costs/  

 with the exact names:  

   aws_july2023.csv  
   gcp_july2023.csv  
   azure_july2023.csv  

 Your goal is to stage those files for analysis, generate a simple cost summary, and keep a minimal run-log of what you did.

 1. Create a new working directory exactly at  
      /home/user/finops/project1  

    Inside it, also create an “archive” sub-directory.  
    (The automated check will look for BOTH directories.)

 2. Copy every “\*_july2023.csv” file from /home/user/raw_costs/ into /home/user/finops/project1/  
    • After a successful copy, move the originals from /home/user/raw_costs/ into  
      /home/user/finops/project1/archive/  

 3. Build a summary report named  
      /home/user/finops/project1/cost_summary.txt  

    Required exact file format (each item is an entire line – **no extra spaces**):  

      Environment CostUSD  
      Prod 2723.56  
      Dev 243.01  

      HighCostServices (>500 USD)  
      Cloud,Service,Environment,CostUSD  
      AWS,RDS,Prod,600.00  
      GCP,BigQuery,Prod,700.00  
      Azure,SQLDB,Prod,650.00  

    Notes for correctness:  
    • “Environment CostUSD” is the header.  
    • The two total lines must be in the order shown: Prod first, Dev second.  
    • After the Dev line there is a single blank line, then the “HighCostServices (>500 USD)” header.  
    • List the three services in the exact order shown.  
    • Numeric values must appear with exactly two decimal places.  

 4. Create a plain-text execution log called  
      /home/user/finops/project1/run.log  

    It must contain **exactly three** lines, in this precise order, with no leading/trailing spaces:  

      STEP: COPY raw CSVs into working directory - RESULT: OK  
      STEP: MOVE original files into archive - RESULT: OK  
      STEP: GENERATED cost_summary.txt - RESULT: OK  

    No timestamps, no extra lines.

 The automated tests will verify:

 • The presence of /home/user/finops/project1/ and its archive sub-directory.  
 • That the three “\*_july2023.csv” files exist in /home/user/finops/project1/ and NOT in /home/user/raw_costs/ after you finish.  
 • That /home/user/finops/project1/archive/ contains those originals.  
 • The exact contents (byte-for-byte) of cost_summary.txt and run.log match the specifications above.

 Accomplish the task entirely from the Linux terminal. Do **not** hard-code absolute costs; calculate them from the CSV data so the procedure would still work if the data changed.
truth:
Existing source files before the task starts:

 1. /home/user/raw_costs/aws_july2023.csv  
    Contents:  
      Cloud,Service,Environment,CostUSD  
      AWS,EC2,Prod,123.45  
      AWS,S3,Dev,45.67  
      AWS,RDS,Prod,600.00  
      AWS,Lambda,Dev,12.34  
      AWS,DynamoDB,Prod,50.00  

 2. /home/user/raw_costs/gcp_july2023.csv  
    Contents:  
      Cloud,Service,Environment,CostUSD  
      GCP,Compute,Prod,200.11  
      GCP,Storage,Dev,50.00  
      GCP,BigQuery,Prod,700.00  
      GCP,CloudFunctions,Dev,30.00  

 3. /home/user/raw_costs/azure_july2023.csv  
    Contents:  
      Cloud,Service,Environment,CostUSD  
      Azure,VM,Prod,400.00  
      Azure,Blob,Dev,80.00  
      Azure,SQLDB,Prod,650.00  
      Azure,Functions,Dev,25.00  

 Expected files AFTER successful task execution:

 A. /home/user/finops/project1/cost_summary.txt  
    Exact nine-line content (with a blank 4th line):

      Environment CostUSD
      Prod 2723.56
      Dev 243.01

      HighCostServices (>500 USD)
      Cloud,Service,Environment,CostUSD
      AWS,RDS,Prod,600.00
      GCP,BigQuery,Prod,700.00
      Azure,SQLDB,Prod,650.00

 B. /home/user/finops/project1/run.log  
    Exact three lines:

      STEP: COPY raw CSVs into working directory - RESULT: OK
      STEP: MOVE original files into archive - RESULT: OK
      STEP: GENERATED cost_summary.txt - RESULT: OK

 C. Directory structure:

   /home/user/finops/project1/                   (exists)  
   /home/user/finops/project1/archive/           (exists)  
   /home/user/finops/project1/aws_july2023.csv   (exists)  
   /home/user/finops/project1/gcp_july2023.csv   (exists)  
   /home/user/finops/project1/azure_july2023.csv (exists)  

   /home/user/finops/project1/archive/aws_july2023.csv   (exists)  
   /home/user/finops/project1/archive/gcp_july2023.csv   (exists)  
   /home/user/finops/project1/archive/azure_july2023.csv (exists)  

   /home/user/raw_costs/   (directory exists but is now empty)

 The automated test will compare the file contents byte-for-byte against those shown above and verify that /home/user/raw_costs/ contains no CSV files.
